etymology.description: 舛 (footsteps) + 囗 (enclosure) -> footsteps around an enclosure -> originally for 圍 (to surround); phonetically borrowed for 'soft leather'
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 舛 (footsteps) and 囗 (enclosure) to depict the idea of footsteps moving around an enclosed area, which was originally associated with the concept of surrounding. This shape was later borrowed for its phonetic value to mean 'soft leather,' as the structural composition resembles a process of encasing or wrapping, similar to the treatment of leather.

examples.chinese: 这件夹克是用韦材料制成的(這件夾克是用韋材料製成的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn jiákè shì yòng wéi cáiliào zhìchéng de.
examples.english: This jacket is made from soft leather material.

examples.chinese: 古代的盔甲常以韦作装饰(古代的盔甲常以韋作裝飾)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de kuījiǎ cháng yǐ wéi zuò zhuāngshì.
examples.english: Ancient armor was often decorated with soft leather.

examples.chinese: 他的书包用韦边制成(他的書包用韋邊製成)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shūbāo yòng wéi biān zhìchéng.
examples.english: His backpack is made with soft leather edges.
